quiz.title: Unit 7 Quiz 12, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Advanced Mole Concept: Molecules, Hydrates, and Mixtures
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Mass of Single Molecules, Hydrated Compounds, Gas Mixtures, Percent Composition, Molar Mass Calculations, Avogadro's Number Applications

questions.question:
What is the mass (in grams) of a single molecule of the compound $C_{60}H_{22}$?

(Atomic masses: $C = 12.011$, $H = 1.008$; Avogadro's Number = $6.022 \times 10^{23}$)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate the molar mass

C: $60 \times 12.011 = 720.66$ g/mol
H: $22 \times 1.008 = 22.176$ g/mol
Total = $720.66 + 22.176 = 742.84$ g/mol

Step 2: Calculate mass of one molecule

Mass = $\frac{742.84}{6.022 \times 10^{23}} = 1.23 \times 10^{-21}$ g

questions.question:
A scientist is using a specialized laser (optical tweezers) to manipulate a single molecule of sucrose. To calculate the forces involved, she needs the exact mass of that one molecule in grams.

What is the mass (in grams) of one single molecule of Sucrose ($C_{12}H_{22}O_{11}$)?

(Atomic masses: $C = 12.01$, $H = 1.01$, $O = 16.00$; Avogadro's Number = $6.022 \times 10^{23}$)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate the molar mass of sucrose

C: $12 \times 12.01 = 144.12$ g/mol
H: $22 \times 1.01 = 22.22$ g/mol
O: $11 \times 16.00 = 176.00$ g/mol
Total = $144.12 + 22.22 + 176.00 = 342.34$ g/mol

Step 2: Calculate mass of one molecule

Mass = $\frac{342.34}{6.022 \times 10^{23}} = 5.68 \times 10^{-22}$ g

questions.question: What is the volume of one mole of a gas at standard temperature and pressure (STP) according to the traditional definition?
questions.explanation: According to the traditional (old) definition of STP (0°C and 1 atm pressure), one mole of any ideal gas occupies 22.4 litres. The IUPAC current standard (0°C and 1 bar) gives 22.7 L, but 22.4 L is the classic value taught in most introductory chemistry courses.

questions.question:
What is the number of moles of water in 600 g of $\text{BaCl}_2 \cdot 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$?

(Atomic masses: $Ba = 137.33$, $Cl = 35.45$, $H = 1.01$, $O = 16.00$)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate molar mass of $\text{BaCl}_2 \cdot 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$

Ba: $137.33$ g/mol
Cl: $2 \times 35.45 = 70.90$ g/mol
$2\text{H}_2\text{O}$: $2 \times 18.02 = 36.04$ g/mol
Total = $137.33 + 70.90 + 36.04 = 244.27$ g/mol

Step 2: Calculate moles of compound

Moles = $600 / 244.27 = 2.456$ mol

Step 3: Calculate moles of water

Each mole of compound contains 2 moles of water
Moles $\text{H}_2\text{O} = 2.456 \times 2 = 4.91$ moles

questions.question:
Copper(II) sulfate pentahydrate, $\text{CuSO}_4 \cdot 5\text{H}_2\text{O}$, is a beautiful blue crystalline solid. When heated, it loses its water and turns into a white powder.

If you have a 125 gram sample of $\text{CuSO}_4 \cdot 5\text{H}_2\text{O}$, how many moles of water are contained within it?

(Atomic masses: $Cu = 63.55$, $S = 32.06$, $O = 16.00$, $H = 1.01$)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate molar mass

Cu: $63.55$ g/mol
S: $32.06$ g/mol
O in CuSO₄: $4 \times 16.00 = 64.00$ g/mol
$5\text{H}_2\text{O}$: $5 \times 18.02 = 90.10$ g/mol
Total = $63.55 + 32.06 + 64.00 + 90.10 = 249.71$ g/mol

Step 2: Calculate moles of compound

Moles = $125 / 249.71 = 0.50$ mol

Step 3: Calculate moles of water

Each mole of compound contains 5 moles of water
Moles $\text{H}_2\text{O} = 0.50 \times 5 = 2.50$ moles

questions.question:
Determine the number of atoms in 28 g of Carbon dioxide ($CO_2$).

(Atomic masses: $C = 12.01$, $O = 16.00$; Avogadro's Number = $6.022 \times 10^{23}$)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate molar mass of $CO_2$

C: $12.01$ g/mol
O: $2 \times 16.00 = 32.00$ g/mol
Total = $44.01$ g/mol

Step 2: Calculate moles

Moles = $28 / 44.01 = 0.636$ mol

Step 3: Calculate molecules

Molecules = $0.636 \times 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 3.83 \times 10^{23}$ molecules

Step 4: Calculate atoms

Each $CO_2$ molecule contains 3 atoms
Atoms = $3.83 \times 10^{23} \times 3 = 1.15 \times 10^{24}$ atoms

questions.question:
What is the percentage of oxygen in $Na_2CO_3$?

(Atomic masses: $Na = 22.99$, $C = 12.01$, $O = 16.00$)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate molar mass of $Na_2CO_3$

Na: $2 \times 22.99 = 45.98$ g/mol
C: $12.01$ g/mol
O: $3 \times 16.00 = 48.00$ g/mol
Total = $45.98 + 12.01 + 48.00 = 105.99$ g/mol

Step 2: Calculate percent oxygen

% O = $(48.00 / 105.99) \times 100 = 45.3\%$

questions.question: What is the equivalence of 1 atomic mass unit (1 amu) in grams?
questions.explanation: 1 atomic mass unit (amu) is defined as exactly $1/12$ the mass of a carbon-12 atom. In grams, 1 amu = $1.66054 \times 10^{-24}$ g. This value is the reciprocal of Avogadro's number: $1/N_A$ grams.

questions.question:
If the weight of a piece of copper is 0.745 g, determine the number of copper atoms present in it in terms of Avogadro's number (N).

(Atomic mass of Cu = 63.55 g/mol)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate moles of copper

Moles = $0.745 / 63.55 = 0.01172$ mol

Step 2: Calculate number of atoms

Number of atoms = moles × N = $0.01172 \times N$

Therefore, the piece contains approximately $0.0117 N$ copper atoms.

questions.question:
What is the weight of a gaseous mixture containing $6.02 \times 10^{23}$ molecules of nitrogen ($N_2$) and $3.01 \times 10^{23}$ molecules of $SO_2$?

(Atomic masses: $N = 14.01$, $S = 32.06$, $O = 16.00$; Avogadro's Number = $6.022 \times 10^{23}$)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate moles of each gas

Moles $N_2 = (6.02 \times 10^{23}) / (6.022 \times 10^{23}) = 1.0$ mol
Moles $SO_2 = (3.01 \times 10^{23}) / (6.022 \times 10^{23}) = 0.5$ mol

Step 2: Calculate mass of $N_2$

Molar mass $N_2 = 2 \times 14.01 = 28.02$ g/mol
Mass $N_2 = 1.0 \times 28.02 = 28.02$ g

Step 3: Calculate mass of $SO_2$

Molar mass $SO_2 = 32.06 + (2 \times 16.00) = 64.06$ g/mol
Mass $SO_2 = 0.5 \times 64.06 = 32.03$ g

Step 4: Calculate total mass

Total = $28.02 + 32.03 = 60.05$ g

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered the most advanced mole concept applications! 🧪🔬
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review hydrated compounds and single molecule calculations.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on step-by-step conversions and unit analysis.
scoreMessages.0: Please review mole concept fundamentals, molar mass calculations, and Avogadro's number applications.
